tweets.text:
Israel is going to totally change the game: 

"Development of Arrow 4 and 5 comes amid a broader global focus on air defense, with countries from the Middle East to Europe racing to produce and purchase high-end missile defense systems and expensive interceptors to go with them.
Germany is the only foreign buyer of the Arrow systems so far, but Patel said Israel has received significant interest from others, as European nations set out to bolster their ballistic missile defenses following years of Russian attacks against Ukraine."

tweets.text:
I don't think enough people are talking about options trading in crypto.

Right now, onchain options are doing around $525M in 7-day notional volume, still very tiny compared to perps.

Derive is essentially the only major protocol focusing on onchain options, capturing 93% of market share.

There's an obvious question of why crypto options haven't reached mainstream yet, despite doing billions of volume in TradFi.

I personally think it's a symbiosis of product fit, initial adoption of perps, and simply visibility.

I bet most people in crypto didn't even know they exist. They just need more adoption, and I think such listings are a good way to get it.

tweets.text:
This is insane.

- There are ~350 "working" chains in crypto.

- Total DeFi TVL is $72 billion.

- 10 chains capture 90% of that.

- Only 14 chains generate >$10,000 in daily fees.

- Only 24 chains generate >$10,000 in daily app fees.

Be ready for 90% of existing chains to shut down in the next 5 years.

tweets.text:
Whenever someone says "the next billion users", we always think of them as humans.

But AI agents will be the heaviest onchain users, as humans are not capable of doing thousands of transactions per minute.

The comparison isn't even close.

Agents are basically restless, as they:

→ Quote and rebalance every few seconds
→ Pay other agents for data and compute
→ Place/pull orders across markets
→ Negotiate/settle with other agents

And much more.

Imagine a market with tens of thousands of these agents. They'll need rails that can handle that kind of volume.

Some chains can already handle heavier loads. @SuiNetwork showed it can push ~6M TPS with the new Tunnels feature.

But the space is still in its early innings for anything close to an active agentic economy.

tweets.text:
Crypto now has almost $15B of Treasury-backed assets that are not just sitting in a private database, but actually circulating onchain.

That is still tiny compared to the actual U.S. Treasury market, which has around $30.9T outstanding, which is a number I can't even fit in my head.

But anyways, it's still roughly 0.05% of the real market being represented in this form, and for crypto, this is already becoming a serious category.

On @RWA_xyz, tokenized treasuries now make up roughly half of all distributed RWA value. And the category is already led by some pretty recognizable names like:

→ @circle USYC
→ @BlackRock BUIDL
→ @OndoFinance USDY

All of them are sitting above $2B.

So IMO, the snapshot shows that one of the biggest RWA categories is becoming more usable onchain, not just more visible onchain.

2026 could be a transformational year. 👀

thread_tweets.text:
One thing that really stands out at $CTV.V / $CTVFF's Campbell-Crotser #fluorspar project: the grade. 👀

Historic drilling outlined 805,841 tons averaging 37.1% CaF₂.

For perspective, major global fluorspar deposits can be economic at far lower grades.

Higher raw ore grade can mean less rock moved and processed per ton of product — and potentially stronger margins.

Now they are going back with the drill bit to test it with modern drilling. ⛏️🔥

🚨 #India is coming for #uranium. ☢️🇮🇳

India plans to grow nuclear capacity more than 11-fold to 100 GW by 2047 — and its largest power producer is already looking to invest in overseas uranium mines to secure future supply.

Read that again.

India isn't just planning reactors. It's preparing to compete for the uranium needed to fuel them.

China. The US. Europe. And now India.

More reactors. More buyers. Same finite pool of uranium.

The uranium supply battle is only beginning. 🔥

thread_tweets.text:
Hollywood has been selling us a future that involves killer AI’s, dead cities or humanity’s grim ending. To be honest, that’s a failure of imagination and is expensive.
Whenever we write about landing on the moon, video calls, or creating things like the submarine we end up even if it's decades later, BUILDING IT!

thread_tweets.text:
This is literally Abundance thinking in action.

What used to take a studio or a VC firm years, greenlighting a film, funding a company, we do in a single evening, in front of the people who can scale it.

The future is a story before it's a fact. We're funding the good ones.

tweets.text:
There's a simple formula underneath every economy on earth:

GDP growth = population growth + productivity growth + debt growth

More workers, more output per worker, or more borrowing. That's all growth has ever been. When the first two stall, debt steps in to fill the gap, which grows the economy now by stealing from the future.

The problem is that the trend rate of growth has been falling for decades, in the US and every developed economy. It now sits around 1.75%.

The reason is the part you can't change. The working-age population is shrinking. Fewer workers means a lower ceiling on growth, no matter what anyone does about it.

So the economy leans harder and harder on the one lever that's left. Debt.

And that's where the real story begins.

tweets.text: All that I've said thus far will be useless if you don't have good governance. Governance is the oversight system that removes the people and the processes if they aren't working well. It is the process that checks and balances power to assure that the principles and interests of the community as a whole are always placed above the interests and power of any individual or faction. Because power will rule, power must be put in the hands of capable people in key roles who have the right values, do their jobs well, and will check and balance the power of others. #principleoftheday
